State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5562          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          327-6444


January 14, 1983


James Rozek
District Counsel
Santa Maria Public Airport
3249 Skyway Drive
Santa Maria, CA 93455

                                          Re: Our File No. A-82-170

Dear Mr. Rozek:

     This letter is to confirm the telephone advice I gave you on January 6, 1983. As you know, the Commission has adopted regulations interpreting AB 1040. These regulations will become effective after they have been approved by the Office of Administrative Law and filed. In the meantime, the staff is using them as guidelines in interpreting AB 1040.

     The regulations interpret the term "legislative body such as a city council" to include all boards or commissions directly elected by the voters. 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18438.1. Under this interpretation, the Santa Maria Airport District would be exempt from the provisions of AB 1040.

     If you have any further questions, please feel free to call me.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [Signature]
                                          Barbara A. Milman
                                          Chief
                                          Legal Division

BAM:km

TELEPHONE 922-1726
AREA CODE 805

SANTA MARIA PUBLIC AIRPORT

3249 SKYWAY DRIVE • SANTA MARIA, CALIFORNIA • 93455

OCT 14 1982

AIRPORT DISTRICT DIRECTORS

CHRISTINE M. CASTELLANOS
PRESIDENT
DANIEL J. DRONENBURG
ELAINE A. HALE, SECRETARY
THOMAS A. LANG
WAYNE E. WARNER

EVERETT W. BERRY
GENERAL MANAGER

JAMES ROZEK, COUNSEL

October 12, 1982

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Barbara A. Milman
Chief of the Legal Division

Dear Mrs. Milman:

Section 84308 was added to the Government Code by Chapter
1049, 1982 Statutes (A.B.1040). Would you please advise, pursuant to Section 83114(b) of the Government Code or otherwise,
whether or not Section 84308 applies to members of the board
of directors (the governing body) of the Santa Maria Public
Airport District, which is an airport district formed and
operating under the California Airport District Act, Public
Utilities Code Sections 22001 and 2200 et seq.

The airport district leases and issues permits, licenses
and other entitlements for use of district's property and
facilities at the Santa Maria Public Airport. Leases (except
month-to-month leases) and licenses are first submitted to the
board of directors for approval. Month-to-month leases and
use permits are granted or issued by the district's staff without specific approval by the directors.

Members of the board of directors are elected by voters
of the district at bi-annual elections. Vacancies on the
board of directors occurring by death or resignation are
filled by appointment of the remaining members of the board.

I would appreciate your advice on the scope and
applicability of Chapter 1049 to the members of the board of
directors of the Santa Maria Public Airport District.

Very truly yours,

James Rozek
District Counsel

cc: General Manager

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5662          322 5660          322 5901          322 6441          322 6444

October 15, 1982


James Rozek
District Counsel
Santa Maria Public Airport
3249 Skyway Drive
Santa Maria, CA  93455

Re: A-82-170

Dear Mr. Rozek,

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been received. If you have any questions
about your advice request, please contact me directly as I
will be responding to your letter.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure
